perturbation.original_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions" and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time. Transporter may file and seek Commission approval under Section 4 of the Natural Gas Act (NGA) at any time and from time to time to change any rates, charges or provisions set forth in the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) and the "General Terms and Conditions" in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, and Transporter shall have the right to place such changes in effect in accordance with the NGA, and this Agreement shall be deemed to include such changes and any such changes Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016 which become effective by operation of law and Commission Order, without prejudice to Shipper's right to protest the same.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. INCORPORATION OF TARIFF INTO AGREEMENT: This Agreement shall incorporate and in all respects be subject to the "General Terms and Conditions". Transporter may file and seek Commission approval under Section 4 of the Natural Gas Act (NGA) at any time and from time to time to change any rates, charges or provisions and Transporter shall have the right to place such changes in effect in accordance with the NGA, and this Agreement shall be deemed to include such changes and any such changes Source: TC PIPELINES LP, 10-K, 2/26/2016 which become effective by operation of law and Commission Order, without prejudice to Shipper's right to protest the same.
perturbation.explanation: I removed the text "and the applicable Rate Schedule (as stated above) set forth in Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Third Revised Volume No. 1, as may be revised from time to time." This is misleading and reduces the clarity of the Rate Schedule's explicit integration.
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the language about the Rate Schedule, the contract becomes less clear regarding how rates are determined and subject to change. This violates the spirit, though not necessarily the letter, of Section 4 of the Natural Gas Act (15 U.S. Code § 717c), which requires just and reasonable rates. Obscuring how these rates are set could lead to disputes about their reasonableness.

Rates and charges (a) Just and reasonable rates and charges All rates and charges made, demanded, or received by any natural-gas company for or in connection with the transportation or sale of natural gas subject to the jurisdiction of the Commission, and all rules and regulations affecting or pertaining to such rates or charges, shall be just and reasonable, and any such rate or charge that is not just and reasonable is declared to be unlawful. (b) Undue preferences and unreasonab
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit mention of the 'applicable Rate Schedule' within the agreement, which directly impacts the clarity of how rates are determined. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing 15 USC 717c, states that all rates and charges must be 'just and reasonable'. By obscuring the Rate Schedule's direct inclusion in the agreement, the contract creates an ambiguity around how rates are set, potentially leading to disputes over whether those rates adhere to the 'just and reasonable' standard mandated by the NGA. This is a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction by potentially obscuring the rate determination process, thus undermining the legal requirement for just and reasonable rates.

perturbation.original_text: 15. NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS: All notices and communications with respect to this Agreement shall be in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means as agreed to by the parties, and sent to the addresses stated below or to any other such address(es) as may be designated in writing by mail, e-mail, or fax, or other means similarly agreed to: ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS Great Lakes Gas Transmission Limited Partnership Commercial Services 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 ANR PIPELINE COMPANY 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 Attn:
perturbation.changed_text: 15. NOTICES AND COMMUNICATIONS: All notices and communications with respect to this Agreement shall be sent to the attention of the Director of Long Term Marketing for both parties, regardless of the subject matter. All notices must be handwritten and delivered in person. ADMINISTRATIVE MATTERS Great Lakes Gas Transmission Limited Partnership Commercial Services 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 ANR PIPELINE COMPANY 700 Louisiana St., Suite 700 Houston, TX 77002-2700 Attn:
perturbation.explanation: This significantly restricts the acceptable forms of communication, requiring all notices to be handwritten and delivered in person. It could potentially impede the ability to provide timely legal notices.
perturbation.law_explanation: While not directly violating the Statute of Frauds (MCL § 566.132) which pertains to what contracts must be in writing, the amendment hinders the practical application of providing notice, potentially frustrating the purpose of requiring written notice under the statute and general contract law principles. Restricting notice to handwritten, in-person delivery is unreasonably burdensome and increases the risk of disputes over proper notification. In general contract law and the statute of frauds, the purpose is that there is a mutual understanding, and this goes against that.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: MCL - Section 566.132 - Michigan Legislature MCL - Section 566.132 Download Section Chapter 566 R.S. of 1846 R-S-1846-566-131-81 Previous Section Next Section Revised Statutes of 1846 (EXCERPT) Chapter 81. Of Fraudulent Conveyances And Contracts, Relative To Goods, Chattels, And Things In Action. 566.132 Agreements, contracts, or promises required to be in writing and signed; enforcement; "financial institution" defined. Sec. 2. (1) In the following cases an agreement, contract, or promise is void unless that agreement, contract, or promise, or a note or memorandum of the agreement, contract, or promise, is in writing and signed with an authorized signature by the party to be charged with the agreement, contract, or promise: (a) An agreement that, by its terms, is not to be performed within 1 year from the making of the agreement. (b) A special promise to answer for the debt, default, or misdoings of another person. (c) An agreement, promise, or undertaking made upon consideration of marriage, except mutual promises to marry. (d) A special promise made by a personal representative to answer damages out of his or her own estate. (e) An agreement, promise, or contract to pay a commission for or upon the sale of an interest in real estate. (f) An assignment of things in action, whether intended as a transfer for sale, for security, or otherwise. (g) An agreement, promise, contract, or warranty of cure relating to medical care or treatment. This subdivision does not affect the right to sue for malpractice or negligence. (2) A person shall not bring an action against a financial institution to enforce any of the following promises or commitments of the financial institution unless the promise or commitment is in writing and signed with an authorized signature by the financial institution: (a) A promise or commitment to lend money, grant or extend credit, or make any other financial accommodation. (b) A promise or commitment to renew, extend, modify, or permit a delay in repayment or performa
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction. By mandating that all notices be handwritten and delivered in person, it unreasonably restricts the means of communication, potentially hindering the ability to provide timely legal notices, conflicting with the Statute of Frauds (MCL § 566.132) principle of ensuring mutual understanding as highlighted in the Law Explanation. While MCL § 566.132, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, primarily focuses on the requirement for certain agreements to be in writing, the change undermines the practical application of providing notice, frustrating the purpose of written notice under general contract law principles.
